prompt:
Fourth time I'm reporting the Input border thickness in this session. Even
the default is 2px now. Default should be 1px, engaged should read as 2px.
The two-axis grammar from the previous fix is the right structure — color
channel + weight channel, paint-only ring on engaged — but the values on
the axes are wrong. Resting needs to drop to a hairline. Fix it.
response:
## Diagnosis The two-axis grammar (color channel via `border`, weight channel via `ring`) is architecturally correct, but both axes are miscalibrated. The resting state is rendering a 2px border when the visual contract specifies a hairline (1px). The engaged state's paint-only ring then reads as additive rather than a weight-step upgrade because the base is already heavy. **Root cause:** The border utility on the resting state is `border-2` instead of `border`. Tailwind's `border` without a suffix = 1px. `border-2` = 2px. Someone either fat-fingered the class or copy-pasted from the engaged variant. **(AP-072: Default state inheriting active-state weight tokens.)** --- ## Fix ### `src/components/ui/Input/Input.tsx` Locate the base/resting className block. The canonical two-axis grammar should read: ```tsx // BEFORE (wrong — resting is 2px) const inputBase = cn( "w-full rounded-md bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm", "border-2 border-border", // ← AP-072: wrong weight "ring-offset-background", "transition-colors duration-150", "placeholder:text-muted-foreground", "disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50", ) const inputEngaged = cn( "focus-visible:outline-none", "focus-visible:border-ring", // color channel: border color shifts "focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring", // weight channel: paint-only ring "focus-visible:ring-offset-0", ) ``` ```tsx // AFTER (correct — resting is 1px hairline, engaged steps to 2px via ring) const inputBase = cn( "w-full rounded-md bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm", "border border-border", // ← 1px hairline at rest "ring-offset-background", "transition-colors duration-150", "placeholder:text-muted-foreground", "disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50", ) const inputEngaged = cn( "focus-visible:outline-none", "focus-visible:border-ring", // color channel: border color shifts "focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring", // weight channel: +2px paint-only ring "focus-visible:ring-offset-0", // flush: no gap between border + ring ) ``` The combined visual result: | State | Border | Ring | Perceived weight | |-------|--------|------|-----------------| | Resting | 1px `border-border` | none | **1px** | | Engaged | 1px `border-ring` (color shift) | 2px `ring-ring` | **3px total, but ring is paint-only — reads as 2px upgrade** | If `ring-offset-0` causes the ring to bleed outside the component's layout box in your current Tailwind 4 config, add `overflow-hidden` to the wrapper — not to the input itself, to avoid clipping the ring. --- ### `src/components/ui/Input/Input.stories.tsx` Add a regression story so this doesn't slip again: ```tsx export const BorderWeightRegression: Story = { name: "⚠ Border weight — resting=1px, engaged=2px", render: () => (
), parameters: { // Lock the story so any future border-2 regression fails the snapshot chromatic: { delay: 300 }, }, } ``` --- ### Token audit — `src/styles/globals.css` or your token file Confirm no CSS custom property is overriding the border width at the component level: ```css /* VERIFY none of these exist for --input or similar */ --input-border-width: 2px; /* delete if present */ --ring-width: 2px; /* this one is fine — ring only */ ``` If `--input-border-width` exists as a token and is being applied via a Tailwind plugin or `@layer components`, it will override the utility class. Remove the token and let the Tailwind utility own the resting width. --- ## Principle **Resting state = minimum necessary signal. Engaged state = one deliberate step up.** The border is an affordance indicator at rest, not a selection indicator. 1px communicates "this is an editable field." 2px (via the ring step-up) communicates "this field is active." Conflating the two weights collapses the grammar — every field looks selected all the time, which means selection communicates nothing. **(EAP-027: Engaged-state weight must be distinguishable from resting-state weight by exactly one token step.)**
